title.purpose: Card titles, dialog headers, section labels. Top of a deliberately shallow scale.

body.purpose: Default reading size across the app.

mono.purpose: Event times and aligned figures, Geist Mono with tabular numerals.

shadows.name: soft
shadows.purpose: Event blocks and lightly-raised elements; a gentle warm-tinted lift.

shadows.name: soft-lg
shadows.purpose: Popovers, dialogs, menus — surfaces that clearly leave the page.

shadows.name: ring-hairline
shadows.purpose: Resting-state card edge. Depth without weight; the default instead of a drop shadow.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for color/border state transitions (~150ms).

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Active-state tactile press on buttons; no hover lift.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override; motion is restrained by default and removed entirely on request.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single committed action on a screen; warm-stone fill, quiet by design.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; height:2rem; padding:0 0.625rem; gap:0.375rem; border:1px solid transparent; border-radius:0.75rem; background:var(--primary); color:var(--primary-foreground); font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1; white-space:nowrap; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform .05s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary), transparent 20%); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform:translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:none; border-color:var(--ring); box-shadow:0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: The workhorse action; carries most of the app so primary stays rare.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; height:2rem; padding:0 0.625rem; gap:0.375rem; border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:0.75rem; background:var(--background); color:var(--foreground); font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1; cursor:pointer; transition:background-color .15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background:var(--muted); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform:translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline:none; border-color:var(--ring); box-shadow:0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Transparent field with a warm hairline border; warm-stone focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { height:2rem; width:100%; min-width:0; border:1px solid var(--input); border-radius:0.75rem; background:transparent; padding:0.25rem 0.625rem; font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; color:var(--foreground); outline:none; transition:border-color .15s, box-shadow .15s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color:var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color:var(--ring); box-shadow:0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat at rest, held by a hairline ring rather than a shadow.
components.html:
This weekend
Two events, one shared.
components.css: .ds-card { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:1rem; border-radius:1.05rem; background:var(--card); color:var(--card-foreground); padding:1rem; font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.875rem; box-shadow:inset 0 0 0 1px color-mix(in oklch, var(--foreground), transparent 90%); } .ds-card__title { font-size:1rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1.375; } .ds-card__body { color:var(--muted-foreground); }

components.name: Badge
components.css: .ds-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.25rem; height:1.25rem; padding:0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius:1.95rem; background:var(--primary); color:var(--primary-foreground); font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:500; line-height:1; white-space:nowrap; }

components.name: Event Block — mine / shared (filled)
components.description: Signature component. Solid fill = mine or shared; white text; soft warm shadow. Shared events carry a Users icon.
components.html:
Dinner with Sam
18:30–20:00
components.css: .ds-event { position:relative; display:flex; flex-direction:column; width:11rem; border-radius:0.6rem; border:1.5px solid color-mix(in oklch, #b45309, black 12%); background:#b45309; color:#fff; padding:0.25rem 0.375rem; font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; text-align:left; box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgb(28 25 23 / 0.06), 0 4px 12px rgb(28 25 23 / 0.06); } .ds-event__row { display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0.25rem; } .ds-event__title { flex:1; font-weight:600; line-height:1.1; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis; white-space:nowrap; } .ds-event__icon { width:0.75rem; height:0.75rem; margin-top:1px; flex-shrink:0; opacity:0.9; } .ds-event__time { font-size:0.6875rem; line-height:1.1; opacity:0.9; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }

components.name: Event Block — partner's (outlined)
components.description: Another person's event: outlined, tinted fill, colored text — 'look, don't touch,' not editable by you.
components.css: .ds-event-outline { position:relative; display:flex; flex-direction:column; width:11rem; border-radius:0.6rem; border:1.5px solid #0f766e; background:color-mix(in oklch, #0f766e, transparent 88%); color:#0f766e; padding:0.25rem 0.375rem; font-family:'Plus Jakarta Sans',ui-sans-serif,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; text-align:left; } .ds-event-outline__title { font-weight:600; line-height:1.1; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis; white-space:nowrap; } .ds-event-outline__time { font-size:0.6875rem; line-height:1.1; opacity:0.9; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }

narrative.overview: Planner is the digital version of a well-kept household almanac that two people write in together: a warm paper surface, exact about dates and times, calm to the touch, and never shouting for attention. It is a tool, not a destination — its job is to let two people answer 'are we both free, and whose is this?' at a glance and then get on with their day. The surface is warm paper with stone ink and a single committed warm-stone accent (terracotta survives only as Member A's identity); warmth is the material, not a decoration layered on top, which is what lets the interface stay quiet without turning cold. Hierarchy is built from a deliberately shallow type scale and generous spacing rather than big display type — a product register, no hero tier, no marketing voice. What it rejects is as defining as what it embraces: not a gamified productivity app, not a generic SaaS dashboard, not a cold minimal mono tool. Quiet and precise, yet human.
keyCharacteristics: Warm, paper-toned light surface; warm-charcoal dark mode — never pure white or pure black.
One committed warm-stone accent, used sparingly; color otherwise carries meaning (whose, what, what-state), with terracotta surviving as Member A.
Shallow type scale, single family in weight contrast; precision over flourish.
Soft warm-tinted shadows and hairline rings; mostly flat, depth only where it means 'floating.'
Rounded, gentle geometry (0.75rem base radius) — approachable, not bubbly.
Accessibility and color-meaning are load-bearing (WCAG AA throughout, never color-only signals).

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Warm stone is the only decorative accent, and it stays rare — primary action, focus, active state, selection. Member/category colors (terracotta = Member A, teal = Member B, amber = shared, the swatch set) carry meaning, not decoration, so they coexist with it. Two stone things competing for 'the' action means one is wrong.

rules.name: The Color-Means-Something Rule
rules.body: Outside the single accent, color is never decorative. A hue answers a question: whose is this (Member A coral / Member B teal), is it ours (shared amber), or what category. Color 'for visual interest' is prohibited.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted warm (stone, not zinc/slate). Pure #000, pure #fff text, and cold grays are forbidden in the default theme.

rules.name: The Shallow-Scale Rule
rules.body: The type scale tops out near 1rem. Hierarchy comes from weight, color, and spacing — not from large type.

rules.name: The Tabular-Time Rule
rules.body: Times and aligned figures are always tabular-nums so a schedule does not jitter as digits change width.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat by default, held by a hairline ring. A shadow appears only when an element is genuinely floating (overlay, dragged event, open menu).

rules.name: The Warm-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadow color is warm stone (28 25 23), never 0 0 0. A cold shadow reads as a foreign element pasted onto the paper.

dos: Do keep the warm-stone accent rare — primary action, focus, active, selection only.
Do let color mean something outside the accent: whose (coral / teal), ours (amber), or category (the swatch set).
Do tint every neutral and every shadow warm; use warm near-black #292524 for text, never #000.
Do build hierarchy from weight, color, and spacing on a shallow scale that tops out near 1rem.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest with a hairline ring; reserve soft shadows for genuinely-floating elements.
Do encode event ownership as filled (mine/shared) vs outlined (theirs), and carry status with texture/outline, not color alone.
Do hold WCAG AA on body and large text in every theme, keep placeholders at full contrast, and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
Do keep tabular-nums on all times and aligned figures.
donts: Don't add gamification — no streaks, badges, confetti, XP, or dopamine nudges.
Don't reach for generic SaaS-dashboard patterns: no card-grid-everything, no gradient accents, no hero-metric templates, no marketing-landing chrome inside the app.
Don't drift cold or sterile: stark black-on-white, zero-warmth, designer-brutalist coldness is the wrong kind of quiet.
Don't use border-left/border-right > 1px as a colored side-stripe; use full borders, a background tint, or a leading icon.
Don't use gradient text or decorative glassmorphism; emphasis comes from weight and size.
Don't introduce a second decorative accent or a display type tier.
Don't ship cold/neutral shadows or pure-gray borders.
Don't convey status, ownership, or errors with color alone.
